title: impatiens engleri subsp. engleri
intro: Impatiens engleri subsp. engleri, also known as the Balsam or Greater Balsam, is a flowering plant native to tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to its high concentration of glycosides and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Impatiens engleri subsp. engleri include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its anti-diarrheal and anti-spasmodic properties. The herbal preparations of this plant often involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from its leaves, flowers, or roots, which can be used to alleviate various health issues.
